extracted_information: The U.S. Domestic Hemp Production Program outlines laboratory testing guidelines for hemp to determine total delta-9 tetrahydrocannabinol (THC) concentration, ensuring compliance with the 0.3% acceptable THC level on a dry weight basis. This includes detailed lab testing standards for methodologies, sample preparation, quality assurance, measurement of uncertainty, and reporting. Compliance protocols cover non-compliant results, retesting, remediation, and information sharing requirements, including submission to the USDA's Hemp eManagement Platform. While the content doesn't detail the specific layout of a Certificate of Analysis (COA), it outlines what results must be reported and how they are shared and retained.

specifications.total_thc_concentration_limit: 0.3% total delta-9 tetrahydrocannabinol (THC) on a dry weight basis
specifications.thc_calculation_method: Post-decarboxylation or similarly reliable methods that consider the potential conversion of delta-9-tetrahydrocannabinolic acid (THCA) into THC.
specifications.measurement_of_uncertainty_mu: Must be estimated and reported with test results. Range reported as a ± value (e.g., +/- 0.05).

sample_preparation.drying: Dry sample until brittle, maintaining THC level; if not possible within 24 hours, hold in freezer at -20°C or lower. Samples dried to a consistent weight (typically 5-12% moisture content).
sample_preparation.grinding: Grind entire sample (leaves, seeds, twigs, stems) for homogeneity.
sample_preparation.specimen_separation: Separate into 'Test Specimen' for analysis and 'Retain Specimen' for reanalysis/confirmation.
sample_preparation.storage: Store samples and extracts in secured locations, in appropriate containers.

lab_quality_standards: Quality assurance protocols ensuring validity and reliability of test results.
Analytical method selection, validation, and verification protocols (fit for purpose).
Protocols for demonstrating testing validity for consistent, accurate analytical performance.
Method performance specifications ensuring sufficient sensitivity.
Effective disposal procedure for non-compliant samples.

pricing.testing_fees: Responsibility of the licensed producer for initial testing and retesting.

temporal_info.dea_registration_deadline: After December 31, 2024, laboratories approved for THC testing must be registered with DEA to handle controlled substances.
temporal_info.record_retention: Laboratories shall retain a legible copy of all official compliance test results for three (3) years from date of analysis. Licensees must maintain a legible copy of remediated test results for three (3) years from receipt.

geographical_data.program_scope: U.S. Domestic Hemp Production Program (USDA, State, Tribal production plans)

references: ISO 17025: General requirements for the complete testing and calibration laboratories (adherence encouraged, not required).
AOAC Standard Method Performance Requirements 2019.003: Quantitation of Cannabinoids in Plant Materials of Hemp (Low THC Varieties Cannabis sp.)
JCGM 100:2008 (GUM): Evaluation of measurement data – Guide to the expression of uncertainty in measurement.
ISO/IEC Guide 98: Expression of Uncertainty in Measurement.
EURACHEM/CITAC Guide 'Quantifying Uncertainty in Analytical Measurement' Second edition (2000).
Food and Drug Administration, Office of Regulatory Affairs, ORA Laboratory Manual Volume III Section 4, Basic Statistics and Data Presentation.
United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime: Recommended Methods for the Identification and Analysis of Cannabis and Cannabis Products.

compliance_protocols.non_compliance_notification: If test results exceed 0.3% THC, the laboratory must promptly notify the producer and the State, Tribal, or Federal regulatory licensing body.

retesting_procedure.method: Follow same procedures as initial test
retesting_procedure.cost: Paid by the licensee requesting the retest
retesting_procedure.reporting: Retest results issued to licensee, copy provided to USDA or its agent.

remediated_hemp_testing.process: Follow same procedures and reporting requirements as initial tests for remediated biomass material.
remediated_hemp_testing.record_keeping: Licensee must maintain a legible copy of remediated test results for three years.

required_recipients: Appropriate State Department of Agriculture or Tribe
information_sharing.submission_platform: All hemp testing laboratories (government, public, or private) shall report test results (passing or failing) to USDA's Hemp eManagement Platform (HeMP).
information_sharing.submission_timing: Lab test results shall be submitted to USDA concurrently when the certificate of analysis is returned to producer and/or State/Tribal regulatory body.
information_sharing.official_compliance_marking: Laboratories shall indicate that a test result is for 'official compliance' purposes. Monitoring results throughout the growing season should NOT be marked as 'official compliance'.
information_sharing.producer_access_to_results: Laboratories may provide results via web portal or electronic mail for easy printing by producers; producers must be able to produce a legible copy upon request for inspection.

compliance_protocols.alternative_testing_protocols: Considered if comparable and similarly reliable, requiring written request to and written approval from USDA.
